response: Japan, with a population of 125 million, has a GDP of $4.2 trillion and exports totaling $756 billion, though its economy is expanding at a modest 1.1% while facing 3.2% inflation and a low unemployment rate of 2.6%. The country carries a substantial debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%, yet its citizens enjoy one of the world’s highest life expectancies at 84.6 years.
